question: Does the adventure verifier library accept replay-grounded reference adventures while rejecting one-at-a-time evidence, causality, route, resource, goal, and metric-firewall tampering?
experimental_unit: one deterministic verifier fixture case built from an independently seeded folded-cavern episode

hypotheses.prediction: Every untampered reference adventure passes every declared hard verifier on discovery, confirmation, and holdout seeds.
Every one-at-a-time adversarial fixture is rejected on every seed.
Each adversarial fixture triggers its predeclared verifier or configuration firewall.
hypotheses.falsifier: A fixture is rejected only for an unrelated reason or not rejected.

model.update_order: Generate a deterministic folded-cavern reference episode, normalize its events, build one valid adventure, apply one declared fixture mutation, then run all hard and diagnostic verifiers.
model.rng: The folded-cavern source consumes numpy.default_rng(seed) at initialization only; verifier execution is pure and consumes no randomness.

seed_plan.pairing: All scenario fixtures for one seed are deep copies of the same valid task, trace, and canonical environment; exactly one declared mutation is applied.

denominators.fixture_detection_rate: detected cases divided by independently seeded cases for the exact fixture class
denominators.valid_acceptance_rate: accepted valid controls divided by valid controls

exposure.eligibility: Every source episode contains three action receipts, a response classification, and a Mitosis Moss deep-probe resurfacing event.
exposure.activation: Every tampered case applies exactly one named mutation to a deep copy of its valid control.
exposure.state_change: The campaign records the mutated task, trace, or environment and the targeted verifier result.

analysis.uncertainty: Complete deterministic fixture enumeration over four source seeds; no tick- or event-level pseudoreplication.
analysis.multiple_comparisons: All nine predeclared tamper classes and the valid control are retained; no verifier champion is selected.
analysis.stopping_rule: Stop after 40 cases, 120 wall seconds, 512 MB RSS, failed source replay, or any expectation mismatch.

budget.execution: Sequential local CPU execution; pure verifier calls; no external models or APIs.
